The purchase consideration was £800,000 cash for the properties and railway, and £13,000 for stocks of minerals and stores taken over. The present output of ore is about 3,000 tons per week, land this is being augmented. A large steel pier is in course of erection by the company at Almeria, and is expected to be completed in January, 1904-. The authorised capital is £450,000, in 4,500 shares of £100 each; all the shares have been issued, 2,500 being fully paid, and 2,000 having £10 paid per share. There is £50,970 part of £150,000 4 per cent. redeemable first mortgage debenture stock, which was offered for public subscription in June, 1900; interest is payable on June 30th and December 31st, and the stock is redeemable at par on October 31st, 1910, but the company may, on giving six months notice, pay 01 stock by drawings at any time after October 31st, 1905, at 102; per cent, or may purchase stock in the open market at any time; in the event of voluntary liquidation the stock is redeemable at 105 per cent; the security is a first charge on the mines, railway and depot land, a floating charge upon all other assets of the company, and a sinking fund by annual payments to debenture trustees equal to Is. per ton on ore from the mines sold or used in the preceding year. It is estimated that the sinking fund will redeem the whole capital of the company during the lifetime of the mines. This company was registered February 10th, 1899, and is a second reconstruction of the Tamworth Gold Mining Co., Ltd., registered February 15th, 1895 see vol. for 1899 . The company took over the Hanging Bock Gold Mine, three miles from Nundle and 40 miles from Tamworth, New South Wales, held direct from Government. A 10-stamp mill is erected, and the mine is let on tribute, but part has been abandoned. An option has been acquired over the Killham Central Tin Mine, at St. Neot, Cornwall. The authorised capital is £100,000, in 400,000 shares of 5s. each ; 281,067 shares are issued and fully paid. There are £2,200 six per ceat. debentures. Tho shares were issued credited as 4s. paid in exohange for fully-paid 5s. shares of the former company, share for share. This company was registered in Western Australia April 26th, 1904, being a reconstruction of a London company of same name registered December 21st, 1900 see vol. for 1904 . The company owns the Camelia Gold Mine, comprising 56 acres, and the Brilliant and Brilliant North, 24 acres, total 80 acres, situate 25 miles from Kanowna, Western Australia ; also two-thirds of the Anagie Lease, and the Kutey Lease, both situate in the Gold Coast Colony, West Africa, formerly the property of the Premier Gold Mines, Ltd. A 10-stamp mill and cyanide plant are erected on the Australian property. Tho authorised capital is £300,000, in 300,000 shares of £1 each ; 129,682 shares are issued and fully paid. The properties include the Barrow Colliery, near BarnBley, Yorkshire, also iron oro mines in the Furness district, Lancashire and In Cumberland. In 1907 the Company entered into a conditional agreement for securing an interest in an iron ore mine in Algeria. The authorised capital is £916,965, in 377 eight per cent, cumulative first preference shares of £45 each £16,965 , 50,000 six per cent, cumulative second preference shares of £4 10s. each £225,000 , and 150,000 ordinary shares of £4 10s. each £675,000 ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. There is £287,600 debenture stock, as below. The capital was originally £150,000 in £100 shares, and was from time to time increased until in 1888 it stood at £2,037,700; it was then reduced to £1,528,275 by writing down each Bhare 25 per cent. ; in 1902, under a re-arrangement of capital in present form, ordinary and second preference shares were reduced from £7 10s. to £4 10s. and first preference from £75 to £45, the latter to receive the same cumulative yearly dividend as formerly, £2,262 j the profits available for dividend are now applied as follows : £2,262 per annum to first preference shores, 6 per cent, cumulative per annum to second pref erenoe and of balance J per cent, on the second preference for every 1 per cent, on ordinary ; the first preference have ten votes, and the second preference and ordinary one vote, per share. Five per cent, debenture stock tor £538,530 was issued, repayable at par July 1st, 1905, or earlier at 102 j £238,530 was redeemed and £287,600 replaced by a new issue in October, 1904, repayable at par Jnly 1st, 1915, or at 102J at any time after July 1st, 1910, on six months notice from company. For 1890 and 1891, dividends of 2 J per cent, were paid on the ordinary shares. For 1892 and 1893 the preference dividends only were paid. Out of the profits of 1896, the first preference dividend for the years 1894, 1895 and 1893, and the second preference dividend for the year 1894, were paid in March, 1897 . Ont of the profits of 1897 the first preference dividend for 1897 and the second pre ference for 1895, were paid in March, 1898 ; out of the profits of 1898 the first preference dividend was paid for 1898 and the second preference for 1896 in March, 1899. In August, 1902, all arrears of preference dividends were paid np, with 4 percent, on reduced ordinary shares. For 1902, 3 per cent, was paid in March, 1903 on the ordinary shares with an extra 1 per oent. on the second preference ; for 1903 only the preference dividends were paid ; for 1904 the first preference and half of the second preference dividend was paid. This oompany was registered August 1st, 1902, to deal with concessions in the Nyasaland Protectorate, and acquired the undertakings of Sharrer s Zambesi Traffic Co., Ltd., and the Nyasaland Estates, and also contracted with the Shire High lands Railway, Nyasaland, Ltd., for construction of the railway from Port Herald to Blantyre, a distance of about 113 miles. The railway was completed in October, 1908, and various other works are being carried out ; concessions of prospecting and mineral rights have been obtained from the British South Africa Co., including the right to select, from four blocks of 500 square miles each, 2,000 reef claims and two large coal areas, and to prospect these for five years ; the company is also interested in certain claims located in Mozambique Teiritory, upon whioh s prospecting mill is being erected. The authorised capital is £1,000 000, in 1,000.000 shares of £1 eaoh; 650,007 shares are issued and fully paid. There are £650,000 five per cent, debentures in bearer bonds of £100 each, £400,000 of which were offered for public subscription in February, 1903, at 96 per cent., andallotted, and the remaining £250,000 is lodged as security for a loan of £200,000 ; the debentures are redeemable at par June 1st, 1930, or earlier at the option of the company, after June 1st, 1907, at 105 per cent., on six months notice ; in event of voluntary liquidation they are repayable at 105 ; interest is payable June 1st and December 1st, bit from June, 1905 pay ment has been agreed to be postponed until June, 1910. In June, 1905, £350,000 five per cent, prior lien debentures were created redeemable at par December 31st, 1933, by means of a cumulative sinking fund commencing October, 1910, or at the oompany s option on six months notice at any time after December, 1910. Interest is payable June 30th and December 31st, bnt from June, 1907, payment has been postponed until June. 1910. The debentures are seoured by trust deed as a speciSc charge on certain debentures of the Shire Highlands Railway Co., and by a floating charge on all other assets of the British Central Africa Co. There is a mortgage for £100,000 ranking in priority to the prior lien debentures. The following are extracts from the prospectus : Particular care has been taken to get this property reported on by the highest authority, and Mr. W. H. Matthews, General Manager of Bayley s Reward Claim, has inspected the mine and supplied a full report as to its value. On completion of the purchase, one of the London directors, who has had practical experience in gold mining, will proceed to the mine in order to supervise the operations and secure the earliest possible attainment of results. Three shafts have been sunk, and considerable development work has been done and is now being carried on to prove the exceptional value of the property. The following telegram was received from the mine on October 2nd, 1H74: Crown Bayley main shaft is down 00ft. The width of reef is 7ft. Have discovered another reef on the property. The shaft Shaft No. 3 see plan of mine has been sunk -10ft. The width of reef in 2ft. The reefs show visible gold. The developments of the mine fully justify the expectations which have been formed.

This company was registered June 25th, 1897, to take over the undertakings of the African Metals Co , Ltd., registered July 12th, 1895 see vol. for 1899 , and the Mines Selection Co., Ltd., registered May 21st, 1895 see vol. for 1899 . The company took over some Witwatersrand deep level claims, farms, Ac, in the Transvaal, and shares in South African, Australasian, Amerioan, and British Columbian companies, including large interest in Brakpan Mines, Ltd., acquired in 1904. The company floated privately the Anglo-Klondyke Mining Co., Ltd. In October, 1905, tho company acquired the Johannesburg interests of Messrs. A. Dunkelsbuhler & Co., for 400,000 fully-paid shares. The authorised capital is £1,200,000, in shares of £1 each ; 1,000,000 shares are issued and fully paid, 210,942 in exchange for Bhares of the old companies. The capital, formerly £300,000, was inoreased to £600,000 in March, 1903, when 200,000 shares were issued at 22s. 6d. each, 150,000 being offered to shareholders, . CONSOLIDATED MINES and 50,000 SELECTION allotted at same price to the Bank fur Handel und Industrie, Berlin, as sole consideration for guaranteeing the entire issae ; the remaining 100,000 were issued in February, 1906, at 30s. In October, 1905, the capital was increased to present amount, 400,000 shares being issued to Messrs. Dunkelsbuhler & Co., and 200,000 held in reserve for conversion of debentures. In November, 1905, £400,000 five per cent, first mortgage debentures to bearer of £20 and £100 each were offered to shareholders at par, the issue being guaranteed by the Bank fur Handol und Industrie at a commission of 2, per cent.; holders may, at any time up to December 31st, 1908, convert into fully-paid shares at £2 per share; debentures may be redeemed after December 31st, 1908, and before December 31st, 1913, at 5 per cent, premium, and are redeemable after 1913 by 15 annual drawings ; the company may at any time purchase deben tures in the market at 105 or less per cent. Dividends paid : For 1897-8, 2s. per share ; 1898-9, 4s. per share ; 1899-00, 4s. per share ; for 1900-1, nil ; 1901-2, 3s. ; 1902-3, 2s. 6d. ; 1904-5, 2s. This company was registered November 1st, 1900, to acquire from the Sheffield Coal Co., Ltd., coal mining leases in Dinnington, Langhton-en-le-Morthen, Todwiok, North and South Anston, near Botherbam, South Yorkshire. The considera tion was £12,500 in fully-paid deferred shares. Shafts have been sunk and the Barnsley coal seam was reached in August, 1904, at 677 yards depth. The output is nearly 2,000 tons per day. The authorised capital is £287,500, in 275,000 ordinary and 12,500 deferred shares of £1 each ; 246 222 ordinary and all the deferred shares are issued and fully paid. The ordinary are first entitled to a cumulative dividend of ten per cent., after which the deferred take a similar dividend, both classes ranking alike for any surplus. The capital was increased from £187,500 in 1906. The Sheepbridge Coal and Iron Co., Ltd., and the Sheffield Coal Co., Ltd., are the chief shareholders. The accounts for year ended June 80th, 1906, submitted in Sheffield Septem ber 24th, 1906, showed cash £26,043, debtors £9,005 ; creditors £15,226, loans and interest £80,166. This company was registered July 15th, 1895, to take over the Dolooath Tfn and Copper Mine situate at Camborne, Cornwall, and held on lease for 60 years from June 17th, 1895, subject to a royalty on the output. The mine was worked extensively in the 18th century principally for copper, and was stopped in 1787. The Dolcoath Mining Co. commenced working on the cost-book system in 1799, and from that time to the end of 1887 the mine produced copper, tin, arsenic, silver, cobalt, Ac, to the value of £3,234,693. For further particulars of the cost-book company, tee vol. for 1900. The mine is being worked by the present company ou a much larger scale than hitherto ; a new vertical shaft the Williams is being sunk. In 1898 the company acquired the Dolcoath West properties, comprising the setts of Cam Camborne, Camborne Vean and Camborne Console. The Elmore plant for treat ing the complex ores has been erected. Output for six months to June 30th, 1906, 49,934 tons crushed produced 920 tons black tin ; to December 31st, 1906, 50,245 tons produced 8934, tons black tin ; to June 30th, 1907, 61,243 tons pro duced 799 tons black tin ; to December 31st, 1907, 49,795 tons produced 772 tons black tin. The authorised capital is £350,000, in 350,000 shares of £1 each ; 840,000 shares are issued and fully paid including 204,617 issued credited as fully paid ; the final oall of 2s. 6d. on 7,828 shares was due September 12th, 1907. The consideration for the property was £75,000, payable according to the prospectus £15,000 in cash, £30,000 in shares, and £30,000 in cash or shares at the option of the directors, but by an agreement of the 21st September, 18&4, the company engaged to pay only £4,000 in cash, and the remaining £71,000 in shares. In August, 1906, was stated that the concession had been confiscated by the Government to alleged insufficient expenditure, the oompany being offered five placer claims on same creek. This company was registered on Jnne 24th, 1902, and is a reconstruction of the Hillgrove Proprietary Mines, Ltd., registered September 22nd, 1897, to acquire from the United Australian Exploration, Ltd., certain gold-mining properties situate at Hillgrove, 22 miles from Armidale, New South Wales. The undertaking of the Cooney Proprietary Mines, Ltd., was afterwards purchased. In 1904, the adjoining property of the Eleanora Gold and Antimony Mining Co., comprising 64 acres, was acquired for £4,000 cash . Total area now held 152 acres ; a 10-Btamp mill is erected on the New Hillgrove section and started crushing in Maroh, 1904, and cyanide plant is also at work. A 15- stamp mill is erected on the Eleanora mine ; both mills are worked by electrical energy transmitted from the Styx Biver. In October, 1905, bursting rook seriously damaged 100 feet of the shaft, which was declared unsafe, and anew vertical shaft has been started 500 feet east of the inoline shaft ; depth at November 4th, 1907, 1,883 feet. During 1905, 3,673 tons orushed yielded £49,029; 1906, 8,814 tons yielded £26,905. Ore reserves at December, 1906: New Hillgrove, 2,438 tons value £39,284; at Eleanora, 25,000 tons value £75,000. The authorised capital is £225.000, in 225,000 shares of £1 each ; 200,007 shares are issued and fully paid. One share credited as 17s. 6d. paid was offered for each old fully- paid £1 share, the whole 200,000 offered being taken up. An interim dividend of Is. per share was paid August 22nd, 1905. The accounts for year ended December 31st, 1905, submitted August 15th, 1906, showed bullion sales £49,028 and a net profit of £5,430, making with balance forward a sum of £19,759 ; dividend and tax absorbed £10,500, leaving a balance of £9,259 carried forward. The accounts to December 31st, 1906, submitted Jnly 5th, 1907, showed bullion sales £30.253, and a loss of £7,059, reduoing credit brought forward to £2,200; cash £8,842, bullion £3,931, debtors £363 j creditor £2,948. The company was promoted by the vendors, General Share and Investment Co., Ltd., who acquired the properties from former company. A main shaft has been sunk the required depth, 325ft. ; driving on the lead of gold-bearing wash is at present suspended ; length of drive or tunnel 900ft., leaving about a further 500ft. to be driven. The authorised capital is £80,000, in 320,000 shares of 5s. each; 201,400 shares £50,350 are issued and fully-paid including 80,000 vendors . There are £15,800 five per cent, first mortgage debentures, redeemable in ten years at 110 per cent., with right to holders to convert into shares at par at any time. The directors have power to increase the share capital to £100,000. This company was registered in the Transvaal in May, 1894, and acquired a number of claims on the dip of the Nigel property, Heidelberg district, South- East Band, Transvaal, the consideration being 190,000 fully-paid shares. In 1895 a further 391 claims were acquired for 205,000 fully-paid shares. In 1899 the company sold 139 southern claims to the Sub-Nigel, Ltd., for 148,250 shares, leaving 613 86 claims now held, all on the farm Varkensfontein, also two water-rights on the farms Varkensfontein No. 217 and Bultfontein No. 28. This company was registered on August 14th, 1902, and is a reconstruction of the North Boulder Gold Mining Co., Ltd., registered July 10th, 1895, to acquire the Eureka Lease No. 35 E, 9 acres, at Ealgoorlie, Western Australia, adjoining the south boundary of the Oroya Brownhill property. An adjoining lease, No. 975 E, 12 acres, was acquired and sold in July, 1896, to the North Boulder East Block, Ltd. see vol. for 1902 , and was repurchased in 1900. In 1902, the adjoining property of the Kalgoorlie Bank of England Gold Mining Co. was purchased for 65,000 fully-paid shares. Total area now held, 32 acres. Crashing commenced in September, 1896, with a Huntingdon mill and a 10-stamp battery, and another Huntingdon mill and cyanide works have been added see below . This company was registered in Melbourne, Viotoria, in Juno 1905, in reconstruc tion of a former colonial company of pamo name registered in 1895. The property comprises leases Nos. 17, 43, 68, 77 and 215, situate at Broken Hill, Barrier Ranges, New South Wales, and yields silver and lead, also zinc. The adjoining property 40 acres of the Victoria Broken Hill Silver Lead Co. see vol. for 1905 has been acquired. The tailings dump has been Bold to the De Bavay Treatment Go., Ltd., also the production of tailings for 11 years from 1906. Reduction plant is at work, and a new shaft is being sunk, depth at August 30th, 1907, 924 feet. A new concentrating mill is being erected and it expee ed to commence work early in 1907. During 22 weeks ended December 31st, 1906, 44,576 tons treated produced 7,372 tons lead, 2,364oz. silver, and 5,648 tons sine; ore reserves at December, 1906, above 800 feet level, 492,000 tons. This company was registered in the Transvaal March 27th, 1895, to acquire from the Randfontein Estates Gold Mining Co., Witwatersrand, Ltd., a block of 311 claims on farms Bandfontein and Uitvalfontein, Western Witwatersrand, Trans vaal. The consideration was £387,500, in fully-paid shares. The property includes the mine formerly worked by the Bandfontein Estates Co., with which a 60-stamp mill and oyanide works in operation were taken over. Early in 1897 the company sold about half its property to the South Bandfontein Co. for 250,000 shares, of which 100,004 are still held. Tho company s mining area now comprises 170 claims. Crushing was commenced on the property by the Bandfontein Estates Co. in September, 1898, with 40 stamps ; an additional 60 stamps were aftorwards added, making 100 now at work, also three tube mills added in 1908 and cvanide plant. During 1907, 175,466 tons yielded £315,185 ; profit 110,504 or 12s. 71d. per ton ; dnring 1908 218,850 tons yielded £343,655 ; estimated profit £120,024. For crushings see table at commencement of volume. Ore reserves at December 31st, 1907, 327,673 tons value ll-8dwts. per ton and 94,883 tons value 4 5dwts. per ton ; at September 30th, 1908, 603,237 tons. The authorised capital is £500,000, in 500,000 shares of £1 each ; all the shares are issued and fully paid. In 1896, 50,000 shares were sold at £2 each. In 1903, £150,000 six per cent, debentures were issued, repayable in twelve annual instalments commencing after three years, or at company s option at any time upon giving six months notice ; a further £100,000 debentures since issued, making £250,000 outstanding at December, 1907, bearing 5 per cent, interest. Dividends declared paid about two months after declaration : For 1895, 10 per cent. ; for 1897, 10 per cent. ; for 1898, 10 per cent. A distribution of 100,000 South Bandfontein snares was announced in January, 1902, being one for every tire Porges shares held on February 5th, 1902 ; shareholders had the option of taking £3 cash instead of the share, making the distribution equal to 12s. cash per Porges share ; since then the following have been declared : for 1905, 10 per cent. ; for 1906, 10 per cent.f 1907, 10 percent. ; 1908. 10 per cent. December 21st, 1908. The accounts for year to December 31st, lt 06, submitted in Johannesburg February 4th, 1907, showed profit for year £109,298, adding balance forward and £10,000 dividends received on South Bandfontein shares made £412,699 ; dividend absorbed £50,000, debenture interest £12,5C0, tax, &c, £11,557, leaving £338,641 credit balance carried forward ; The accounts to December 31st, 1907, submitted in Johannesburg, February IS A 436 . POHOES RANDFONTEIN 7th, 1908, showed receipts £315,185 and a profit of £110,504, making with balance forward and accrued dividends a sum of £459,146 ; dividend December, 1907 absorbed £50,000, debenture interest £12 500, tax £8,779, sundries £55, leaving £387,812 carried forward ; shareholdings £200,000. A share register is kept in London. The 11th November, 1896, was appointed settling day in shares Nob. 1 to 487,500 and September 10th, 1908, in Nob. 487,501 to 500,000. This company was registered in the Transvaal December 1st, 1902, and owns the entire freehold farm Elandsfontein, No. 85, comprising 3,800 acres, situate in the Pretoria district of the Transvaal, with the Premier Diamond Mine thereon. The original vendors received 67,000 fully-paid shares for part of the farm with mine and £25,650 cash has since been paid for the remainder of the freehold. In 1904, the company acquired the adjoining farm LouwBbaken, No. 399, area 1,931 morgen, and in 1905 a portion of Farm Spitzkop, No. 31, 829 morgen, and portion of farm Znikerboschfontein, No. 325, 1,136 morgen, were acquired. In September, 1908, the property of the Pretoria District Diamond Co., Ltd., comprising 176 morgen adjoining on the west, was acquired for £21,650. The mine covers an area of abont 3,570 claims j the blue ground has been proved by a borehole to 1,000ft. depth ; plant to treat 24,500 loads per day has been erected. Under Transvaal law, the Government is entitled to six-tenths of tho profits from the mine, after repayment of the company s capital expenditure. December, 1902, to October, 1903 ... 78,931 ... 99,208 ... 1 20 ... £102, 63 Tear ended October, 1904 ... 939,265 ... 749,653 ... 798 ... £867,738 1906 ... 1,388,071 ... 815,652 ... -609 ... £622,081 1906 ... 2,988,171 ... 899,746 ... 301 ... £073.3111 1907 ... 6,538,669 ... 1,889,987 ... -290 ... £805,729 1908 ... 7,836,328 ... 2,078,825 ... -286 ... £789,000 Extract from chairman s speech at meeting held February 25th, 1908: The Trails of the mine appear to be vertical, this may be due to the nature of the rim-rock, which appears to be uniform in hardness, and the surface is therefore not displaced to the same extent as in other diamond mines, and this fact enables us to work this mine as an open cast to h very considerable depth. The rim-rook so far as can be judged is not likely to give trouble from falls of reef to any appreciable extent,and theopen working might therefore be to say 1,200 or 1,500 feet or more, which will represent approximately 500,000,000 loads. Taking 400,000,000 loads only, this gives you 26 years in the open, or at the present rate of working, say, 50 years. As the mine increases in depth it will be necessary to sink a shaft outside the rimrock, and to drive a tunnel from the shaft to the open mine. This cost will not be neoessary for some time to come, as the present system of working can be to a depth of at least 100 feet, which would represent approximately 136,000,000 loads, or say, 9 years work. Phone: 2321 and 2322 Wall. This company was registered January 12th, 1895, to acquire the undertaking of the Rhodesia Development and Exploration Synd., Ltd. The company has been con cerned in the notation of the Bulawayo Market, Chieago-Gaika, Rhodesia Matabeleland, Gaika Gold, Davies Selukwe, Lomagunda Development, Loinagunda Reefs, Mayfair Development, Scottish Mashoualand, Ayrshire Gold, Rovue Mauicaland, Grand Champion, Edmutidian and other companies. In 1897 the assets of the South African Investors Synd., Ltd., were purchased for 6,000 fully-paid shares. The agency business carried on by the Gold Fields of Matabeleland, Ltd., in Rhodesia, has also been acquired. In 1903, the assets of the Filabnsi Charterland Goldfields, Ltd., were purchased for 1,000 shares. In December, 1906, it was decided to extend the company s operations outside Africa, Properties held at 30th Juno; 1907 : Claims owned 370, partly owned 280 j land owned 50,412 morgen, also interest in a further 92,304 mcrgen, equal in all toabout 3 4,750 acres ; 3 building stands in Salisbury.S in Bulawayo and 1 in T&ti ; various shares, including large holdings in the Consolidated African Copper Trust, Ayrshire Gold, Rhodesian Banket and West Rhodesian Banket companies. The authorised capital is £280,000, in 280.000 shares of £1 each ; 264,231 shares are issued and fully paid. The capital was formerly £100,000 ; 16,411 reserve shares were offered to shareholders in 1898, at £5 5s. each and 1,532 were sold at £5 Is. 9d. The capital was increased to £150,000 in 1899, and to 175,000 in 1901 ; 25,000 shares were issued to shareholders at £4 10s. each in 1900, and 27,135 at £4 10s. in 1901. In 1902, the capital was increased to £225,000, the 50,000 new shares being offered to shareholders at £5 each; and in 1904 increased to £252,000, and subsequently to present amount. This company was first formed in 1836, and was registered with limited liability on the 24th March, 1871. The properties consist of iron and steel works and collieries, the mineral rights covering 2,500 acres, in South Wales ; also a brewery ; several new pits are being sunk. Tbe revenue is now chiefly derived from the collieries j the manufacture of steel has been abandoned. The chief pits are known as Groesfan, Mardy, Penygarreg, Penguin, and New Duffryn. Coal output for 1904-5, 783,531 tons ; for 1905-6, 798,230 tons, 1906-7, 829,042 tons. The authorised capital is £1,000,000, in 200,000 shares of £5 each ; all the shares have been issued and are deemed to be fully paid, subject to conditions as below respecting 73,062 shares Nos. 126,939 to 200,000 , called new shares, which were issued with £2 10s. per share credited as paid. The capital was originally £650,000, in 10,000 shares of £50 and 10,000 shares of £15, but was increased in November, 1881, to its present amount by the creation of 70,000 new shares of £5, the old shares being subdivided into £5 shares at the same time. The new shares were offered, with 3,062 unissued old shares, to shareholders pro rota with the sum of £2 10s. per share credited as paid thereon, the final instalment of 5s. of the £2 l Os. cash paid thereon making fully-paid being due on June 1st, 1898. The 1question dealing with tho possible liability on these shares issued as £2 10s. paid or £2 10s. discount has been submitted to counsel, and in 1899 special resolutions were passed to treat the Bhares as fully paid, but making the £2 10s. per share reserve capital to be called up only in the event of winding up, and giving holders of discount shares prior rights for return of capital to tho extent of any portion of the £2 10s. por share so called np ; for this purpose a special Act of Parliament has been obtained. There are 6 per cent, mortgage debentures amounting to £314,000 in bonds of £20, £100, and £500, balance of £350,000 issued in exchange for six and seven per cent, bonds outstanding, and forming a floating charge on the property, interest being payable half-yearly on the 30th June and 31st December. The bonds run for 99 years from January 1st, 1887, and are redeemable at par by annual drawings of £4,000 in June which commenced in 1899 , for payment on the following December 31st ; after redeeming £4,000 in December, 1907, £314,000 remained. In Jannary, 1906, £150,000 part of £200,000 five per cent, first mortgage new pits debenture stock in not less than £10 fractions was offered for public 61 . KHTMNBY IRON subscription and allotted ; interest payable 1st May and 1st November ; took may be repaid by the oompany at 105 per cent, after 10 years, by giving six months notice, and will also be redeemable at same rate in the event of voluntary liquidation for purpose of reconstruction or amalgamation. An interim dividend is, when available, declared in January, the maximum rate of which i8 limited to the rate sanctioned by the shareholders at the previous annual meeting. ROBERT ADDIE £5 10s. each and 15,000 shares of £9 each; all the shares are issued and fully paid The original capital was £250,000 in 10,000 preference and 15,000 ordinary shares of £10 each, the preference being offered for public subscrition in January, 1893; it was reduced in 1899 to £235,000 by writing off £1 per ordinary share loss on collieries abandoned, and in December, 1900, the preference capital was reduced from £100,000 to £70,000 by paying off £3 per share, and on May 31, 1913, a further sum of £1 105. was repaid on each preference share, making capital as above. Dividends In addition to a cumulative preferential dividend of 6 per cent. per annum the preference shares were entitled. so long as the reference share capital stood at £100,000, to one-third of the divisible profit remaining over a ter payment of a non-cumulative dividend of 6 per cent. on the ordinary shares, but in February, 1907, it was resolved that the residue be divisible between both classes pro min. To provide for the gradual reduction of the preference share capital, there is to be set aside annually as a special reserve out of the profits remaining over after payment of the preference dividend, and before any dividend is paid on the ordina shares, a sum of £5,000, to be applied in the rateable reduction of the preference ca ital, suzb annual payment being cumulative. The company may not create or issue any de ntures or debenture stock, or any preference shares to rank either preferably to or pan panu with the preference shares nowissued, without the consent of the holders of three-fourths of the preference capital. Registered January 9, 1906, in the Transvaal, and has acquired a 25 years, lease, with right of renewal for a further 25 years, over 500 acres, on Farm Damplaats, No. 319, 20 miles north-east of Boshof. Orange Free State. The consideration was £9,000 cash and £132,500 in fully-paid shares In October, 1906, the adjoining farm Rietkuil, No. 269, 2,335 morgen, was acquired for £8,636, and in 1907 the freehold farm Waaihoek No. 102, 2,446 morgen on the southern bank of the Modder River and five morgen of the freehold farm Waterval on the northern side of the river, on which is erected the water pumping station. Prospect ing rights are also held over the remainder of Farm Damplaats having an area of 11,238 acres. The Government is entitled to four-tenths of profits, but until December, 1915, this has been commuted for a fixed payment of £100 per month. Owing to contraction of the pipe coupled with low average yield, work on the old mine has ceased, and operations directed to developing two new areas, situate to the south-west of the old mine, the combined areas of which are equal to about 50 claims. Registered September 19, 1894, in the Transvaal, as the Robinson Deep, Ltd., to acquire from the Goldfields Deep, Ltd., about 148 main reef dip claims south of the Robinson Mine, Witwatersrand, Transvaal. A further 86 claims on the dip of the Worcester and Ferreira mines were afterwards purchased from the Paarl Ophir Co., for £10,000 cash, and £25,000 in fully-paid shares. The company was reconstructed and registered in the Transvaal under the present title on July 15, 1899. In January, 1910, 1 03 claims were acquired for £36,703, and early in 1911, undermining rights covered by an area equal to 5 27 claims were acquired from the Government. The considera tion was £28,998 per annum for a period of ten years, commencing September, 1911. As from October 1, 1913, £2,181 per month out of profits is to be placed to a reserve gold account for the purpose of amortisation of the said claims. Crushing commenced in April. 1898, with 40 stamps; 260 have since been added, making 300 stamps in all; ten tube mills and cyanide and slimes plant are also erected. This company was registered in tho Transvaal November 9th, 1895, to acquire various mining properties and interests, chiefly in the Transvaal. The purchase consideration was £125,000, in fully-paid shares. Some properties have been disposed of and others acquired. Properties now owned include the following : One-half undivided interest m the freehold and proelaimedBuitenf ontein portion of the goldbearing farm Middelvlei No. 610, in extent 737 morgen, 302 sq. roods, on which are situated two raynpachton, 271 morgen, 270 sq. roods im area, equivalent to 300 8 claims, also a further 200 claims adjoining, recently acquired by the company; this ground is situated on the western extension of tho Randfontein and Main Reef series, Witwatersrand ; the freehold brings in an annual revenue of from £500 to £600 in half licences. A one-eighth interest in a portion of the mynpacht, on farm Turffontein No. 198, comprising 26 morgen on the deep horizon of the Main Reef series contained in the Turf Club and Village Deep, and a similar interest in a water right 1,000 by 50 feot in the Wemmer pan, situate on farm Turffontein No. 219. The freehold farm Springbokkuil No. 292, situated in the Standcrton district, Transvaal, 1.767 morgen in extent. A three-fourths undivided interest in the gold-ljearing farm Naauwkloof m the Rustenburg district, Transvaal, in extent 483 morgen. A three-fourths undivided interest in a freehold manganese property at Houts Bay, near Cape Town, area 18 morgen, 15 sq. roodB. A one-eighth undivided interest in the Wisbeach Swazieland Mineral Concession, granted by King Umbandine, and recognised by the British Government. The Middlevlei claims are to be prospected by boreholes when conditions are favourable for commencing work. The company is at present carrying on an investment business and holds various mining shares. The authorised capital is £250,000, in 250,000 shares of £1 each ; 226,000 shares are issued and fully paid. This company was registered March 1st, 1904, to acquire from the Compaiiis Minora de Mexico, Ltd., options on a gold and silver mining property known as Kspejeras and adjoining claims, total area about 200 acres, situate 95 miles north-east of the city of Mexico and 2 miles south of Tetela de Ocampo, State of Puobla, Mexico. The consideration was £60,000 in fully-paid shares. The com pany was promoted by the vondors. A mill commenced work in February, 1906, but work is at present suspended, and the property is to be worked on tribute or sold. Registered January 21, 1907, to acquire alluvial tin-bearing properties, area 454 acres, in the Kinta district of the Federated Malay States. The consideration was £30,000, payable £15,000 cash and £15,000 in fully-paid shares. During 1908, a further 28 acres were acquired, also two blocks for dumping purposes. Eight monitors are at work. Output of tin for year ending January 31, 1913, 435 tons; to January 31, 1914, 358 tons; to January 31. 1915. 494 tons. In 1910, 1,996 acres of alluvial tin property, near Taiping, Perak, were acquired for 5,000 fully-paid shares and 5,000 shares were offered to shareholders at £2 each to provide the necessary working capital. A further 50 acres were acquired during 1911 making a total of 2,046 acres. Two gravel pumps were erected and the property tested and in September, 1913, Tekka-Taiping, Ltd., was formed to work the property, the consideration being 40,000 fully-paid shares with the right to subscribe for 25,000 shares at par, and in October, 1913, shareholders in Tekka, Ltd., were offered the right to subscribe for these shares. Of the 40,000 shares received for the property, 20,000 were distributed pro rate to Tekka shareholders, being one for every four held. Capital £90,000, in shares of £1 each; 80,000 shares are issued and fully paid. Tsla company was registered on January 21st, 1882, to acquire the Adjsh Bippo gold-mining property, about 1,800 acres in extent, situate in the Wassau District, Gold Coast Colony, West Africa. The purchase consideration was £75,000, payable £33,300 in fully-paid shares and £41,700 in cash. In 1894 the adjoining. Cinnamon Bippo property 820 acres , formerly owned by the Cinnamon Bippo Gold Mine, Limited, was purchased by Messrs. Crocker, Swauzy and Cleaver who were the principal vendors to this company , and handed over gratis. The reef-bearing portion of the property is equal to about 2,133 mining claims, and under agreements made with the Consolidated Gold Fields of South Africa, Limited, and South African Gold Trust, Limited, in March, 1900, the New Gold Coast Agency, Limited, is dealing with certain portions; in any subsidiary companies formed the Wossnn Company receives practically half the vendors shares and has the right to subscribe for half the working capital. The Adjah Bippo Deep, Limited, and Cinnamon Bippo, Limited, have been formed by the New Gold Coast Agency, Limited. The company retains and is working the Adjah Bippo Mine, equal to about 166 claims. Twenty heavy stamps and 12 light stamps are erected on the Adjsh Bippo Mine. During the your 1896, 4,027 tons yielded 6,9590z. of gold, an average of 102. litidwt. per ton. In 1897, 5,799 tons yielded 7,255oz. of gold. In 1898 ten months crushing , 4,162 tons yielded 4,62602. of standard gold, equal l lloz. per ton. Crushing was suspended in November, 1898; a new shaft has been sunk and a new lo-stamp mill and cyanide plant are being erected. The authorised capital is £260,000, in 260,000 shares of £1 each; 246,300 Ihsres have been issued and are fully paid. The capital was increased from £100,000 to £120,000 in 1897, and in February, 1899, was further increased to £150,000, when shareholders took up 20,000 new shares at £1 10s. escb. On March 23rd, 1900, it was resolved to increase the capital to its present amount to provide reserve shares against conversion of debentures as below, and an option on 60,000 shares at £2 each was given for two years. - An issue of £120,000 six per cent. first mortgage debentures in £50 bonds at par took place in April, 1900, holders having the right at any time before April 5th, 1902, to exchange their bonds for fully-paid shares at £2 per share ; the whole of the bonds have now been extinguished. A first dividend of la. per share was declared in May, 1897. The accounts to December 31st, 1902, submitted on March 3rd, 1903, showed cash,£14,715; loans, to brokers, £30,900; investments at cost, £73,747 chiefly Console ; debtors, £651 ; creditors, £16,085 ; profit and loss credit balance, £6.176 as in previous accounts . Vendors shares held but not valued in accounts, 8,000 Adjah Bippo Deep and 2,800 Cinnamon Bippo. The 9th January, 1901, was appointed a special settling day in debentures. This company was registered in Edinburgh July 14th, 1876, as the Clyde Coal Co., Ltd., to carry on coal mining operations at Hamilton, in Scotland. Tn 1892 the company acquired the Business and collieries ef John Wilson, when the name of the company was changed. In 1894 the compauy acquired the lease of a coalfield on the estate of Glencraig, near Lochgelly, Fifeshire. The collieries are known as Shawfield, Brownlee, Law, Gillhead, Douglas Park, E. Farkhead, Clyde, and Glencraig Fife , covering 3,350 acres; also Crosshill Estates, adjoining Glencraig, acquired in 1902. The company has also acquired the lease of the Saline and Outh coalfields, Fifeshire, about 3,100 acres, near the new Kosyth Naval Base ; a railway three miles long has been constructed to develop this property. Plant is being erected, and the Outh portion has been tested by boreholes. The authorised capital is £230,000, in 4,000 seven per cent, cumulative first preference and 1,000 seven per cent, cumulative second preference shares of £10 each and 60,000 ordinary shares of £3 each j all the shares are issued, 1,791 first preference and 60,000 ordinary being fully paid, and 2,209 first preference and all the second preference having £5 called, making the paid-up capital £213,955. The capital, originally £150,000, subsequently increased to £190,000, in preference and ordinary shares, was reduced in 1884 to £85,000 by writing off £7 per ordinary share, and the preference dividend was reduced from 10 to 7 per cent. In 1892 the capital was increased to £170,000 by creation of the second preference and 25,000 new ordinary shares. The second preference were allotted to shareholders at par, and the new ordinary at £2 premium. In 1894 the capital was increased to present amount by creation and issue of 20,000 new ordinary shares to share holders. This company was registered in the Transvaal on the 29th November, 1886, to acquire the farm Driefontein, Eastern Witwatersrand, 10,355 acres in extent, with a mynpacht of 300 claims. The vendors received £45,000 in cash and £150,000 in shares. In January, 1889, the company sold 36 claims for £210,000 in cash and £15,000 in shares of the Balmoral Main Reef Gold Mining Co., Ltd. In May, 1893, 12 claims adjoining the Glencairn property, with the corresponding 48 dip claims, making 60 in all, were sold to Barnato Bros, for £60,000. The company now owns Mynpacht No. 320, 296 morgen in extent, 78 prospecting and two diggers claims and 4,780 morgen freehold on farm Drietfontein, No. 148, Boksburg district, Transvaal, and also derives a revenue of about £11,000 per annum from a residential estate of 4,892 acres, situate on the said farm to the north of the main reef series. Crushing commenced in January, 1888, with a 50-stamp mill, and further 50 stamps were afterwards added, but crushing was suspended in 1891 and the mine has since been thoroughly developed and a new 120-stamp mill and cyanide plant erected, crushing being resumed in 1896. A further 100 stamps making 220 and slimes plant have been added and are now at work. During 1906, 374,000 tons yielded gold value£501,044 or 26s. 9-5d. per ton, at a cost of 19s. -ld. and a profit of 7s. 5 3d. per ton ; during 1907. 402,100 tons yielded nold value £538,272 ; estimated working profit £194,150.
